SEARCH
Package compiler.ast.expression

Class SensorNode

java.lang.Object
compiler.ast.ASTNode
compiler.ast.expression.Expression
compiler.ast.expression.SensorNode
All Implemented Interfaces:
Expr
public class SensorNode extends Expression
Represents a sensor query expression in the AST. Sensor nodes query the critter's state or environment to obtain values for use in conditions and expressions. Supported sensors are:
  • nearby[direction]: check what's nearby in a direction (0-5 for hexagon)
  • ahead[distance]: check what's ahead at a given distance
  • random[max]: generate random integer in [0, max)
  • smell: detect odors (no argument)
Most sensors require an argument (index). The SMELL sensor is special: it has no argument. Examples:
  • nearby[0] = 1 (check if there's food nearby)
  • ahead[2] != 0 (check if there's something 2 steps ahead)
  • random[100] < 50 (50% chance condition)
  • smell (check for odors)
See Also:
  • NumberNode
  • MemoryNode
  • BinaryExpr
  • Expression

  • Constructor Details

    • SensorNode

      public SensorNode(TokenType sensorType, Expression argument, int line, int column)
      Creates a sensor query node.
      Parameters:
      sensorType - the sensor keyword (NEARBY, AHEAD, RANDOM, or SMELL)
      argument - the index/max argument (non-null for most sensors, null for SMELL)
      line - source line where the sensor appears
      column - source column where the sensor appears

  • Method Details

    • getSensorType

      public TokenType getSensorType()
      Returns the sensor type.
      Returns:
      the TokenType (NEARBY, AHEAD, RANDOM, or SMELL)

    • getArgument

      public Expression getArgument()
      Returns the optional argument expression.
      Returns:
      the argument expression, or null if this sensor has no argument (SMELL)

    • hasArgument

      public boolean hasArgument()
      Checks whether this sensor query has an argument. Only SMELL sensors have no argument; all others return true.
      Returns:
      true if an argument is present; false for SMELL sensor

    • getChildren

      public List<ASTNode> getChildren()
      Description copied from class: ASTNode
      Returns the immediate children of this node in source order. This method is used by tree traversal algorithms (printing, analysis, transforms) to navigate the AST structure. The order of children mirrors their appearance in source code.
      Specified by:
      getChildren in class ASTNode
      Returns:
      list of direct child nodes (may be empty, never null)

    • accept

      public <T> T accept(ASTVisitor<T> visitor)
      Description copied from class: ASTNode
      Accepts a visitor to perform an operation on this node. This implements the visitor design pattern, allowing new operations to be added without modifying the AST node classes. The visitor pattern enables clean separation between tree structure and algorithms (printing, analysis, code generation, etc.).
      Specified by:
      accept in class ASTNode
      Type Parameters:
      T - the return type produced by the visitor
      Parameters:
      visitor - the visitor to apply (non-null)
      Returns:
      the result produced by the visitor's visit method for this node type
